global.title: nullable input types require explicit coalescing to match state types
global.content:
# nullable input types require explicit coalescing to match state types

Optional input types and optional state types have different nullability:

- **Input**: `InputMaybe<T>` = `null | undefined | T`
- **State**: `Maybe<T>` = `T | null`

TypeScript won't accept `undefined` where `null` is expected, so you must coalesce:

```typescript
// ❌ Type error — undefined not assignable to T | null
amount: action.input.amount,
notes: action.input.notes,

// ✅ Correct — coalesce to match Maybe<T>
amount: action.input.amount || null,
notes: action.input.notes || [],
```

Since [[truthy checks are safer than undefined checks for optional state assignment]], prefer `if (action.input.field)` over `if (action.input.field !== undefined)` for conditional updates.

---

Topics:
- [[reducer patterns]]
- [[gotchas]]
global.description: InputMaybe<T> allows null | undefined | T but state uses Maybe<T> = T | null, so coalesce with || null or || [] to satisfy TypeScript
